real_goal: Allow users to reset passwords safely without refactoring auth provider internals
mvp_scope: Reset token generation and validation only
must_not_do: Do not rewrite the auth provider
Do not build admin reset flows
Do not add a full email delivery abstraction

module_boundaries.responsibility: Generate, validate, expire, and consume reset tokens
Expose the minimum request and confirmation endpoints

selected_path: Write a focused failing test for expired reset tokens
Implement minimal expiry validation in the reset token path
Add a focused regression test for consumed tokens

tdd_micro_loops.stop_condition: stop if auth provider refactor becomes necessary

tdd_micro_loops.red: Used reset token cannot be reused
tdd_micro_loops.green: Mark token as consumed after successful reset
tdd_micro_loops.stop_condition: stop if endpoint redesign becomes necessary

max_scope: one validation module and one focused test file
stop_condition: Stop if implementation requires auth provider refactor
handoff: Execute this brief. After execution, produce an Evidence Ledger.

context_budget_contract.scope_boundary: Implement reset-token validation only. Do not modify auth provider internals.
context_budget_contract.violation_handling: Stop if the fix requires auth provider refactor or more than three touched files. Split into a smaller task.

context_diet_map.reason: Task only touches reset-token validation, not OAuth provider internals.

failure_to_smaller_task_protocol.failure_split_rule: If implementation requires auth provider refactor, stop and split into a smaller seam task.
failure_to_smaller_task_protocol.smaller_fallback_task: Extract reset-token validation behind a minimal seam before adding endpoint or email delivery.
new_verification: Focused unit test for reset-token expiry
Focused unit test for consumed-token rejection
